rules.Darkvision: See in total darkness up to stated range (B&W).
rules.Low-light vision: See 2× as far as humans in dim light.
rules.Scent: Detect creatures by smell (30 ft; 60 ft downwind; 15 ft upwind); pinpoints within 5 ft.
rules.Tremorsense: Detect vibrations in contact with ground up to N ft.
rules.Blindsight: Perceive surroundings without vision up to N ft.
rules.Damage Reduction (DR N/type): Reduce damage by N unless weapon has bypass type (magic, silver, cold iron, adamantine, alignment, etc.)
rules.Spell Resistance (SR N): Attacker must beat d20+caster level vs. SR to affect with spell.
rules.Energy Resistance N (type): Reduce that energy type damage by N per attack.
rules.Energy Immunity: Completely immune to that energy type.
rules.Fast Healing N: Regain N HP per round automatically (does not work if dead or at 0).
rules.Regeneration N: As fast healing but also regrows lost limbs; certain damage (often fire/acid) deals lethal even to this creature.
rules.Poison: Two saves required (Fort); initial and secondary effect (usually 1 min apart).
rules.Disease: Fort save or contract; incubation period then recurring damage.
rules.Paralysis: Fort save or paralyzed for duration.
rules.Petrification: Fort save or turned to stone.
rules.Swallow Whole: Melee attack; grapple check; swallowed take crushing+acid each round; can cut free from inside.
rules.Half-Dragon: +2 STR, +2 INT/CHA; gain energy immunity; breath weapon; NA +4; LA +3
rules.Half-Fiend: +4 STR/CON, +2 INT/WIS/CHA; SR 11+HD; DR 5/magic; spell-like abilities; wings; LA +4
rules.Half-Celestial: +4 STR/CON, +2 INT/WIS/CHA; SR 11+HD; DR 5/magic; spell-like abilities; wings; LA +4
rules.Vampire: Undead type; +6 STR, +2 DEX, +4 INT/WIS/CHA; +8 NA; energy drain; blood drain; dominate; mist form; weaknesses (sunlight, running water, stake); LA +8
rules.Lich: Undead type; +2 INT/WIS/CHA; touch paralysis; phylactery; CR +2; LA +4
rules.Skeleton: Undead; lose all; gain DR 5/bludgeoning; darkvision 60 ft; CR varies
rules.Zombie: Undead; lose all; gain DR 5/slashing; single damage type; CR varies
rules.Adding a template: Apply stat changes, gain/lose abilities, recalculate CR.

body:
# Monsters — D&D 3.5 SRD Reference

Source: d20srd.org/srd/monsters/
Monster URL: https://www.d20srd.org/srd/monsters/[monsterName].htm

---

## Stat Block Format (Standard)

```
[Monster Name]
Size/Type: [Size] [Type] [(Subtype)]
Hit Dice: [N]d[HD]+[CON bon] ([avg HP] hp)
Initiative: +[mod]
Speed: [N] ft. (N squares)[, fly N ft. (maneuverability)]
AC: [N] ([breakdown]), touch [N], flat-footed [N]
Base Attack/Grapple: +[BAB]/+[grapple]
Attack: [weapon] +[mod] mel/rng ([damage]/[crit])
Full Attack: [all attacks]
Space/Reach: [N] ft./[N] ft.
Special Attacks: [list]
Special Qualities: [list]
Saves: Fort +[N], Ref +[N], Will +[N]
Abilities: Str [N], Dex [N], Con [N], Int [N], Wis [N], Cha [N]
Skills: [skill] +[N], ...
Feats: [list]
Environment: [terrain]
Organization: [solitary/pair/group (N–N)]
CR: [N]
Treasure: [type]
Alignment: [alignment]
Advancement: [N–N HD (Size)]
Level Adjustment: [N or —]
```

---

## Creature Types and Key Immunities

| Type | HD | BAB | Good Saves | Key Immunities/Features |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Aberration | d8 | ¾ | Will | — |
| Animal | d8 | ¾ | Fort, Ref | No language; low Int |
| Construct | d10 | Full | — | No Con; immune to mind/disease/poison/sleep/stun; can't heal naturally |
| Dragon | d12 | Full | Fort, Ref, Will | Immune to sleep/paralysis; low-light/darkvision |
| Elemental | d8 | Full | Varies | Immune to poison/sleep/paralysis/stun; not flanked |
| Fey | d6 | ¾ | Ref, Will | Low-light vision |
| Giant | d8 | ¾ | Fort | — |
| Humanoid | d8 | ¾ | Varies | — |
| Magical Beast | d10 | Full | Fort, Ref | Low-light, darkvision |
| Monstrous Humanoid | d8 | Full | Ref, Will | Darkvision 60 ft |
| Ooze | d10 | ¾ | — | Blind; immune to mind/gaze/visual; no Dex bon to AC |
| Outsider | d8 | Full | Fort, Ref, Will | Darkvision 60 ft; don't need to eat/sleep |
| Plant | d8 | ¾ | Fort | Immune to mind/poison/sleep/paralysis/stun/polymorph |
| Undead | d12 | ¾ | Will | No Con; immune to mind/sleep/paralysis/stun/disease/poison; CR: turn check |
| Vermin | d8 | ¾ | Fort | Mindless (Int —); immune to mind effects |

---

## Common Monster Special Abilities

**Darkvision**: See in total darkness up to stated range (B&W).
**Low-light vision**: See 2× as far as humans in dim light.
**Scent**: Detect creatures by smell (30 ft; 60 ft downwind; 15 ft upwind); pinpoints within 5 ft.
**Tremorsense**: Detect vibrations in contact with ground up to N ft.
**Blindsight**: Perceive surroundings without vision up to N ft.

**DR (DR N/type)**: Reduce damage by N unless weapon has bypass type (magic, silver, cold iron, adamantine, alignment, etc.)
**SR (SR N)**: Attacker must beat d20+CL vs. SR to affect with spell.
**Energy Resistance N (type)**: Reduce that energy type damage by N per attack.
**Energy Immunity**: Completely immune to that energy type.
**Fast Healing N**: Regain N HP /rnd automatically (does not work if dead or at 0).
**Regeneration N**: As fast healing but also regrows lost limbs; certain damage (often fire/acid) deals lethal even to this creature.

**Poison**: Two saves required (Fort); initial and secondary effect (usually 1 min apart).
**Disease**: Fort save or contract; incubation period then recurring damage.
**Paralysis**: Fort save or paralyzed for duration.
**Petrification**: Fort save or turned to stone.
**Swallow Whole**: mel attack; grapple check; swallowed take crushing+acid each round; can cut free from inside.

---

## CR Quick Reference (Common Monsters)

| CR | Monster Examples |
|---|---|
| 1/2 | Kobold, goblin, skeleton (human) |
| 1 | Zombie, hobgoblin, giant rat, dire rat |
| 2 | Gnoll, orc warrior, ghoul, giant spider |
| 3 | Bugbear, ogre (CR 3), werewolf, doppelganger |
| 4 | Gargoyle, wereboar, ghast, minotaur |
| 5 | Troll, weretiger, shadow, wraith |
| 6 | Wyvern, hill giant, ettin, mind flayer (CR 8) |
| 7 | Stone giant, lamia, manticore |
| 8 | Frost giant, young adult dragon (varies by color/age) |
| 9 | Cloud giant, fire giant |
| 10 | Adult blue dragon, lich (CR varies) |
| 12 | Storm giant, vampire |
| 15 | Ancient red dragon, balor |
| 20+ | Pit fiend, elder wyrm, tarrasque (CR 20) |

---

## Dragons — Age Categories and CR

| Age Category | Size | CR mod | Key SR | Breath Weapon |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Wyrmling | Small | Base | 15 | 2d dice |
| Very Young | Medium | Base+1 | 16 | 4d dice |
| Young | Medium | Base+2 | 17 | 6d dice |
| Juvenile | Large | Base+3 | 18 | 8d dice |
| Young Adult | Large | Base+4 | 19 | 10d dice |
| Adult | Large | Base+5 | 20 | 12d dice |
| Mature Adult | Huge | Base+6 | 21 | 14d dice |
| Old | Huge | Base+7 | 22 | 16d dice |
| Very Old | Huge | Base+8 | 23 | 18d dice |
| Ancient | Huge | Base+9 | 24 | 20d dice |
| Wyrm | Gargantuan | Base+10 | 25 | 22d dice |
| Great Wyrm | Colossal | Base+11 | 26 | 24d dice |

**Base CRs** (adult = CR): Red/Blue 13, Green 11, Black 9, White 8, Gold 15, Silver 14, Brass 10, Bronze 13, Copper 11

---

## Templates

**Half-Dragon**: +2 STR, +2 INT/CHA; gain energy immunity; breath weapon; NA +4; LA +3
**Half-Fiend**: +4 STR/CON, +2 INT/WIS/CHA; SR 11+HD; DR 5/magic; Sp abilities; wings; LA +4
**Half-Celestial**: +4 STR/CON, +2 INT/WIS/CHA; SR 11+HD; DR 5/magic; Sp abilities; wings; LA +4
**Vampire**: Undead type; +6 STR, +2 DEX, +4 INT/WIS/CHA; +8 NA; energy drain; blood drain; dominate; mist form; weaknesses (sunlight, running water, stake); LA +8
**Lich**: Undead type; +2 INT/WIS/CHA; touch paralysis; phylactery; CR +2; LA +4
**Skeleton**: Undead; lose all; gain DR 5/bludgeoning; darkvision 60 ft; CR varies
**Zombie**: Undead; lose all; gain DR 5/slashing; single damage type; CR varies

**Adding a template**: Apply stat changes, gain/lose abilities, recalculate CR.

---

## Encounter Distance (Surprise Rules)

| Terrain | Encounter Distance |
|---|---|
| Dungeon | 2d6 × 5 ft |
| Scrubland / forest (thick) | 2d8 × 10 ft |
| Open plains | 6d6 × 10 ft |
| Jungle | 2d4 × 5 ft |
| Underground cavern | 3d6 × 10 ft |
| Desert | 6d6 × 10 ft |

Perception (Listen/Spot) vs. Stealth (Hide/Move Silently) determines surprise.
